description: Rows in Alex Gorbenko's reference workbook (deal_details_Alex_2026.xlsx) that Alex himself flagged as wrong, or that violate a basic structural invariant (duplicate BidderIDs, out-of-order sequencing). This file documents Alex's own annotations on his own work. It is NOT a list of scoring exclusions — Alex's workbook is a reference guideline, not ground truth. When the AI extraction diverges from one of these rows, the AI is often more correct. Each divergence gets a human-adjudicated verdict during development (see reference/alex/README.md §Diff contract). Decisions about how to handle these rows during the xlsx-to-JSON conversion live in rules/dates.md §Q.

saks.reason: Unsolicited letter from Company H; no NDA, no price, no further contact. Alex's own comment: 'Should be deleted.'
Sponsor A/E row. Alex's own comment: 'Not a separate bid, should be deleted.'

zep.reason: Five bidders collapsed into a single row. Alex's own comment: 'This field needs to be expanded to 5 bidders; one of them bid 20, another 22, another three [20,22]?'

mac-gray.reason: BidderID=21 duplicates row 6957. Structural invariant violation (P-D2: BidderID unique within deal).

medivation.reason: BidderID=5 duplicates row 6070. Also out-of-order: ID=5 on 7/19 vs row 6067's ID=4 on 8/8. Structural invariant violations (P-D2 uniqueness, P-D3 date ordering).
Aggregated NDA row: BidderName='Several parties, including Sanofi' (bid_note=NDA, rough=2016-07-05). Compresses 3+ entities (Sanofi + >=2 unnamed parties) into one row; violates E1 atomization.
Aggregated Drop row: BidderName='Several parties' (bid_note=Drop, 2016-08-20). Companion to row 6065; violates E1 atomization.
